steps.title: Map the two-part structure
steps.content: The first part says innovation studies overemphasize examples that suggest technology always becomes more complex. The wheel-ceramics example represents that familiar progress-toward-complexity story. The second part presents Haddad's gold metallurgy research as an exception, showing a Bronze Age case where sophisticated techniques declined.

steps.title: Choose the counterexample frame
steps.content: The text moves from a repeated interpretation to a counterexample showing that simpler processes can replace advanced methods. That is exactly the role of a counterexample: it limits an overbroad interpretation rather than disproving all technological development. A invents several origin studies, B adds controversy, and D says Haddad is criticized. The answer is C.
